verses.latin_vulgate: Noli lætari, Israël ;  noli exsultare sicut populi :  quia fornicatus es a Deo tuo ;  dilexisti mercedem super omnes areas tritici.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תִּשְׂמַ֨ח יִשְׂרָאֵ֤ל ׀ אֶל־גִּיל֙ כָּעַמִּ֔ים כִּ֥י זָנִ֖יתָ מֵעַ֣ל אֱלֹהֶ֑יךָ אָהַ֣בְתָּ אֶתְנָ֔ן עַ֖ל כׇּל־גׇּרְנ֥וֹת דָּגָֽן׃
verses.king_james: Rejoice not, O Israel, for joy, as other people: for thou hast gone a whoring from thy God, thou hast loved a reward upon every cornfloor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Area et torcular non pascet eos,  et vinum mentietur eis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גֹּ֥רֶן וָיֶ֖קֶב לֹ֣א יִרְעֵ֑ם וְתִיר֖וֹשׁ יְכַ֥חֶשׁ בָּֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: The floor and the winepress shall not feed them, and the new wine shall fail in her.

verses.latin_vulgate: non habitabunt in terra Domini.  Reversus est Ephraim in Ægyptum,  et in Assyriis pollutum comedit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֥א יֵשְׁב֖וּ בְּאֶ֣רֶץ יְהֹוָ֑ה וְשָׁ֤ב אֶפְרַ֙יִם֙ מִצְרַ֔יִם וּבְאַשּׁ֖וּר טָמֵ֥א יֹאכֵֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: They shall not dwell in the Lord’s land; but Ephraim shall return to Egypt, and they shall eat unclean things in Assyria.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non libabunt Domino vinum,  et non placebunt ei.  Sacrificia eorum quasi panis lugentium ;  omnes qui comedent eum, contaminabuntur :  quia panis eorum animæ ipsorum :  non intrabit in domum Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־יִסְּכ֨וּ לַיהֹוָ֥ה ׀ יַ֘יִן֮ וְלֹ֣א יֶעֶרְבוּ־לוֹ֒ זִבְחֵיהֶ֗ם כְּלֶ֤חֶם אוֹנִים֙ לָהֶ֔ם כׇּל־אֹכְלָ֖יו יִטַּמָּ֑אוּ כִּֽי־לַחְמָ֣ם לְנַפְשָׁ֔ם לֹ֥א יָב֖וֹא בֵּ֥ית יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: They shall not offer wine offerings to the Lord, neither shall they be pleasing unto him: their sacrifices shall be unto them as the bread of mourners; all that eat thereof shall be polluted: for their bread for their soul shall not come into the house of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid facietis in die solemni,  in die festivitatis Domini ?
verses.king_james: What will ye do in the solemn day, and in the day of the feast of the Lord?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce enim profecti sunt a vastitate :  Ægyptus congregabit eos ;  Memphis sepeliet eos :  desiderabile argentum eorum urtica hæreditabit,  lappa in tabernaculis eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־הִנֵּ֤ה הָֽלְכוּ֙ מִשֹּׁ֔ד מִצְרַ֥יִם תְּקַבְּצֵ֖ם מֹ֣ף תְּקַבְּרֵ֑ם מַחְמַ֣ד לְכַסְפָּ֗ם קִמּוֹשׂ֙ יִֽירָשֵׁ֔ם ח֖וֹחַ בְּאׇהֳלֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: For, lo, they are gone because of destruction: Egypt shall gather them up, Memphis shall bury them: the pleasant places for their silver, nettles shall possess them: thorns shall be in their tabernacles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venerunt dies visitationis,  venerunt dies retributionis.  Scitote, Israël,  stultum prophetam,  insanum virum spiritualem,  propter multitudinem iniquitatis tuæ,  et multitudinem amentiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּ֣אוּ ׀ יְמֵ֣י הַפְּקֻדָּ֗ה בָּ֚אוּ יְמֵ֣י הַשִּׁלֻּ֔ם יֵדְע֖וּ יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל אֱוִ֣יל הַנָּבִ֗יא מְשֻׁגָּע֙ אִ֣ישׁ הָר֔וּחַ עַ֚ל רֹ֣ב עֲוֺנְךָ֔ וְרַבָּ֖ה מַשְׂטֵמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The days of visitation are come, the days of recompence are come; Israel shall know it: the prophet is a fool, the spiritual man is mad, for the multitude of thine iniquity, and the great hatred.

verses.latin_vulgate: Speculator Ephraim cum Deo meo,  propheta laqueus ruinæ factus est super omnes vias ejus ;  insania in domo Dei ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צֹפֶ֥ה אֶפְרַ֖יִם עִם־אֱלֹהָ֑י נָבִ֞יא פַּ֤ח יָקוֹשׁ֙ עַל־כׇּל־דְּרָכָ֔יו מַשְׂטֵמָ֖ה בְּבֵ֥ית אֱלֹהָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: The watchman of Ephraim was with my God: but the prophet is a snare of a fowler in all his ways, and hatred in the house of his God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Profunde peccaverunt, sicut in diebus Gabaa.  Recordabitur iniquitatis eorum,  et visitabit peccata eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֶעְמִ֥יקוּ שִׁחֵ֖תוּ כִּימֵ֣י הַגִּבְעָ֑ה יִזְכּ֣וֹר עֲוֺנָ֔ם יִפְק֖וֹד חַטֹּאותָֽם׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: They have deeply corrupted themselves, as in the days of Gibeah: therefore he will remember their iniquity, he will visit their sins.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quasi uvas in deserto inveni Israël,  quasi prima poma ficulneæ in cacumine ejus vidi patres eorum :  ipsi autem intraverunt ad Beelphegor,  et abalienati sunt in confusionem,  et facti sunt abominabiles sicut ea quæ dilexerunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּעֲנָבִ֣ים בַּמִּדְבָּ֗ר מָצָ֙אתִי֙ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל כְּבִכּוּרָ֤הֿ בִתְאֵנָה֙ בְּרֵ֣אשִׁיתָ֔הּ רָאִ֖יתִי אֲבוֹתֵיכֶ֑ם הֵ֜מָּה בָּ֣אוּ בַֽעַל־פְּע֗וֹר וַיִּנָּֽזְרוּ֙ לַבֹּ֔שֶׁת וַיִּהְי֥וּ שִׁקּוּצִ֖ים כְּאׇהֳבָֽם׃
verses.king_james: I found Israel like grapes in the wilderness; I saw your fathers as the firstripe in the fig tree at her first time: but they went to Baal–peor, and separated themselves unto that shame; and their abominations were according as they loved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ephraim quasi avis avolavit ;  gloria eorum a partu, et ab utero, et a conceptu.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶפְרַ֕יִם כָּע֖וֹף יִתְעוֹפֵ֣ף כְּבוֹדָ֑ם מִלֵּדָ֥ה וּמִבֶּ֖טֶן וּמֵהֵרָיֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: As for Ephraim, their glory shall fly away like a bird, from the birth, and from the womb, and from the conception.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod etsi enutrierint filios suos,  absque liberis eos faciam in hominibus ;  sed et væ eis cum recessero ab eis !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י אִם־יְגַדְּלוּ֙ אֶת־בְּנֵיהֶ֔ם וְשִׁכַּלְתִּ֖ים מֵאָדָ֑ם כִּֽי־גַם־א֥וֹי לָהֶ֖ם בְּשׂוּרִ֥י מֵהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Though they bring up their children, yet will I bereave them, that there shall not be a man left: yea, woe also to them when I depart from them!

verses.latin_vulgate: Ephraim, ut vidi, Tyrus erat fundata in pulchritudine ;  et Ephraim educet ad interfectorem filios suos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶפְרַ֛יִם כַּאֲשֶׁר־רָאִ֥יתִי לְצ֖וֹר שְׁתוּלָ֣ה בְנָוֶ֑ה וְאֶפְרַ֕יִם לְהוֹצִ֥יא אֶל־הֹרֵ֖ג בָּנָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: Ephraim, as I saw Tyrus, is planted in a pleasant place: but Ephraim shall bring forth his children to the murderer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Da eis, Domine. Quid dabis eis ?  da eis vulvam sine liberis, et ubera arentia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תֵּן־לָהֶ֥ם יְהֹוָ֖ה מַה־תִּתֵּ֑ן תֵּן־לָהֶם֙ רֶ֣חֶם מַשְׁכִּ֔יל וְשָׁדַ֖יִם צֹמְקִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Give them, O Lord: what wilt thou give? give them a miscarrying womb and dry breasts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes nequitiæ eorum in Galgal,  quia ibi exosos habui eos.  Propter malitiam adinventionum eorum,  de domo mea ejiciam eos ;  non addam ut diligam eos :  omnes principes eorum recedentes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־רָעָתָ֤ם בַּגִּלְגָּל֙ כִּי־שָׁ֣ם שְׂנֵאתִ֔ים עַ֚ל רֹ֣עַ מַעַלְלֵיהֶ֔ם מִבֵּיתִ֖י אֲגָרְשֵׁ֑ם לֹ֤א אוֹסֵף֙ אַהֲבָתָ֔ם כׇּל־שָׂרֵיהֶ֖ם סֹרְרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: All their wickedness is in Gilgal: for there I hated them: for the wickedness of their doings I will drive them out of mine house, I will love them no more: all their princes are revolters.

verses.latin_vulgate: Percussus est Ephraim ;  radix eorum exsiccata est :  fructum nequaquam facient,  quod etsi genuerint,  interficiam amantissima uteri eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֻכָּ֣ה אֶפְרַ֔יִם שׇׁרְשָׁ֥ם יָבֵ֖שׁ פְּרִ֣י (בלי) [בַֽל־]יַעֲשׂ֑וּן גַּ֚ם כִּ֣י יֵלֵד֔וּן וְהֵמַתִּ֖י מַחֲמַדֵּ֥י בִטְנָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Ephraim is smitten, their root is dried up, they shall bear no fruit: yea, though they bring forth, yet will I slay even the beloved fruit of their womb.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abjiciet eos Deus meus, quia non audierunt eum,  et erunt vagi in nationibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִמְאָסֵ֣ם אֱלֹהַ֔י כִּ֛י לֹ֥א שָׁמְע֖וּ ל֑וֹ וְיִֽהְי֥וּ נֹדְדִ֖ים בַּגּוֹיִֽם׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: My God will cast them away, because they did not hearken unto him: and they shall be wanderers among the nations.
